analysis.consistency: The spell description is internally consistent, detailing the area of effect, duration, components, and specific magical effects generated within the warded area. The mechanics of the sub-effects (corridors, doors, stairs, and other spell effects) are clearly defined, often referencing existing spells for mechanical clarity.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is comprehensive, covering setup choices (specifying unaffected individuals/passwords) and detailing multiple layers of defensive measures. The use of references to other spells (e.g., _arcane lock_, _web_, _suggestion_) is standard and effective for conveying complex mechanical effects concisely.

mechanics.area_of_effect: Up to 2,500 square feet (e.g., 50 ft square or equivalent division), up to 20 ft tall.
key_mechanics: Area manipulation (heavily obscured fog in corridors, direction misdirection).
Door locking (like _arcane lock_) and illusion options.
Web creation on stairs (like _web_) that regrows if destroyed.
Choice of secondary effects, including limited placements of _dancing lights_ (programmable), _magic mouth_, _stinking cloud_, _gust of wind_, or _suggestion_.
Dispel resistance: Dispel Magic only removes one chosen effect, not the entire ward.
Permanency option: Casting daily for one year creates a permanent ward.
potential_points_of_interest: The cost and quantity of material components are quite high, suggesting a major investment.
The limitations on the secondary effects (e.g., only four _dancing lights_, two _magic mouths_) require careful tactical placement.
The corridor effect causing creatures to believe they are going the opposite direction is a strong navigational defense.
